title: Financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund
subtitle: The total financial support provided to developing countries for climate change mitigation and adaptation. Countries have set a collective goal of mobilizing $100 billion per year from 2020 onwards.
caption:
Financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund

The total financial support provided to developing countries for climate change mitigation and adaptation. Countries have set a collective goal of mobilizing $100 billion per year from 2020 onwards.

The line chart tracks the annual volume of financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund from 2011 to 2022, measured in current US dollars per year. The vertical axis ranges from $0 to $80 billion, with gridlines marking $10 billion intervals. The data series, labeled "World," begins in 2011 at a value just under $30 billion and remains flat through 2012. A visible increase occurs in 2013, where the line rises to approximately $40 billion. The trend continues upward through 2016, reaching just above $50 billion, before experiencing a slight dip in 2017 to just under $50 billion. The line recovers in 2018 to roughly $55 billion and holds steady through 2020. From 2020 to 2022, the chart shows a steep upward trajectory, with the value rising from the low $50 billion range to a peak of $80 billion in the final year. The subtitle notes a collective goal of mobilizing $100 billion per year from 2020 onwards; the data point for 2022 sits at the $80 billion mark, which is the highest value recorded on the chart. The overall pattern displays a general increase in funding over the twelve-year period, with the most rapid growth occurring in the final two years.
